content.assessment: This proposal closes a specific gap left by state-sector-only levers by extending segregation-impact gatekeeping across all maintainer types, including the church/private sector where growth and admissions discretion are most strongly linked to increasing segregation (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-07). It is preventive rather than remedial, so its equity impact is bounded to marginal future expansion.
opportunities: If consistently applied, could slow further growth of the church-sector selective share that has roughly doubled its share of secondary schools since 2010, curbing a documented segregation-amplifying trend (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-07)
Could be combined with contraction levers (T2) to jointly limit both state and non-state selective growth
strengths: Directly targets the maintainer segment (church/private) where segregation-amplifying growth is most strongly documented and least covered by other proposed levers (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-07)

Builds on a documented causal channel (Encs case pattern) linking track-expansion approval to local creaming of higher-status pupils from feeder schools, giving the mechanism direct empirical grounding relevant to segregation dynamics
Cross-maintainer reach addresses a structural blind spot common to T1 and T2, which are largely confined to state-sector levers
threats: Inconsistent or politically selective application could allow segregation-generating expansions to continue in politically favored cases, undermining equity gains
Roma segregation, already elevated and growing independent of school-choice dynamics, would not be directly remedied by blocking future expansion alone (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-06)
weaknesses: Purely preventive scope means it does not reduce segregation from already-approved tracks, leaving the existing SES-skewed access pattern in currently operating selective schools untouched (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-02)

Does not address geographic access disparities or within-school classroom sorting, which operate independently of expansion approvals (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-08, F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-10)
